name: AutoAnimate (Zero-Config Add/Remove/Move Animations)

performance.notes: Uses WAAPI (Web Animations API); minimal overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Zero-config animation for adding/removing/reordering DOM elements. One line: useAutoAnimate(ref) in React, or autoAnimate(el) in vanilla JS. Vue/Svelte/Solid plugins. Works on any parent element — children automatically animate in/out/move. ~2.5KB. Custom animation functions supported. MutationObserver-based. Perfect for lists, tabs, accordions, conditional rendering. No layout shift jank. 'Framer Motion for people who don't want to think about animation' — just add it and it works.

gotchas: Only animates direct children of the parent element
No control over stagger timing for complex choreography
Can conflict with other animation libraries on same element
best_when: Tabs, accordions, and conditional UI sections
Any parent element whose children change dynamically
Teams wanting animation with zero configuration
avoid_when: Animating the element itself (not its children)
summary: AutoAnimate adds smooth add/remove/reorder animations with one line of code. useAutoAnimate(ref) in React, autoAnimate(el) vanilla. Works in React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, vanilla JS. ~2.5KB. MutationObserver watches children and auto-animates transitions. Perfect for lists, tabs, accordions. Zero configuration. Use Framer Motion or GSAP for complex choreography — AutoAnimate is for effortless basic transitions.
